behavior_summary: Provides the object's response when the player 'looks at' (examines) the oak table: it displays a single short label/string 'table ' and then suspends the U8P_OAKTBLNS::look process.
Acts as a minimal/placeholder examine handler rather than a descriptive or interactive script — there are no menus, conditions, or external calls.
The process ends immediately after showing the bark, so no state is changed and no further interactions are offered.
notable_control_flow: U8P_OAKTBLNS::look executes as a process (processType 0x0208) triggered by the engine's U8P_OAKTBLNS::look/examine action.
The flow is linear: show a Bark (the short text 'table ') then suspend/terminate the process.
There are two low-confidence Unknown nodes present in the raw which appear to be structural remnants; they do not introduce branching or additional behavior.
open_questions: Is the short text 'table ' a complete label or a placeholder for a longer resource string stored elsewhere?
Was the minimal response chosen intentionally (object design) or as an artifact of incomplete script/data extraction?
Does the engine supply an implicit context/actor parameter to the U8P_OAKTBLNS::look process here (implicit 'this' or player reference) even though no explicit parameters are declared?
